samples.xml_description:
<module id="edge_detect">
    <description>Implement a module for edge detection.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Reset signal (active low).</input>
        <input id="a">Input signal.</input>
        <output id="rise">Output signal indicating a rising edge.</output>
        <output id="down">Output signal indicating a falling edge.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter id="0">State 0: Previous value of a is 0.</parameter>
        <parameter id="1">State 1: Previous value of a is 1.</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <reg id="state" type="sequential_logic" width_description="[0:0]">Current state.</reg>
        <reg id="next_state" type="sequential_logic" width_description="[0:0]">Next state.</reg>
        <reg id="rise" type="sequential_logic" width_description="[0:0]">Rising edge detected.</reg>
        <reg id="down" type="sequential_logic" width_description="[0:0]">Falling edge detected.</reg>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        The edge_detect module detects rising and falling edges in the input signal a and generates corresponding output signals rise and down. 
        It maintains the previous value of a in the state variable and compares it with the current value of a to detect edges. 
        If a rising edge is detected, the rise output signal is set to 1. If a falling edge is detected, the down output signal is set to 1. 
        The state is updated on the positive edge of the clock signal. If the reset signal is low, the state is set to 0. 
        The outputs rise and down are set to 0 after detecting an edge to avoid continuous assertion. 
    </implementation>
</module>
